title: Neonauclea synkorynes
intro: Neonauclea synkorynes, also known as the "Synkorynes Neonauclea", is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neonauclea synkorynes include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Neonauclea synkorynes is typically prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is often used to treat fever and malaria, as well as to alleviate symptoms of rheumatism and digestive discomfort.
taxonomy: Neonauclea synkorynes belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of around 13,000 species and is one of the largest families of flowering plants. Neonauclea synkorynes is also classified as a member of the tribe Naucleeae, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. Further, Neonauclea synkorynes is classified within the genus Neonauclea, which includes around 70 species of flowering plants. The classification of Neonauclea synkorynes is still being researched and may be subject to revision as new information becomes available.
